purpose: The commit adds a check in hci_cmd_sync_queue to detect the HCI_UNREGISTER flag; when the flag is set the function returns an error instead of queuing the command, preventing a use‑after‑free that would occur after hci_unregister_dev frees the device.
Removes an unnecessary inclusion of asm/termios.h from n_hdlc.c, relying on linux/tty.h (which already pulls linux/termios.h) to simplify the include hierarchy.
The commit updates the perf c2c documentation to include the newly added 'N/A' metric for store operations, ensuring the report table description matches the code.

The patch fixes incorrect handling of the hugetlb VMA lock during VMA splitting and range unmapping. It allocates a new lock for duplicated VMAs to avoid double free, corrects a race in hugetlb_unmap_file_folio, changes __unmap_hugepage_range_final to free the lock instead of clearing VM_MAYSHARE, and makes the presence of a VMA lock a prerequisite for PMD sharing.
Adds documentation of the AVDD and MICVDD power supplies to the device‑tree binding for the Realtek rt5682s codec, updating the dt‑bindings file to reflect supplies already used by a board DTS.
The commit adds a missing iounmap() call in the zorro7xx_remove_one() function to release a resource that was allocated with ioremap() during probe, fixing a resource leak.
The commit removes an unused 'work' field from the struct mlx5e_async_ctx in the mlx5e driver, which is a straightforward code cleanup without altering functionality.

The commit changes the type of cfg80211_auth_request::key_idx from unsigned to signed so that the sentinel value -1 can be stored without triggering static analysis warnings. This is a straightforward code-quality adjustment.

The commit reorders the entries of CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in the RISC‑V defconfig files so that they are sorted in the same way savedefconfig would produce, without altering any functional behavior.

The commit adds a helper function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() to the MCP251xfd CAN driver and replaces existing inline checks for CAN-FD mode with calls to this function, improving code readability and maintainability.
The commit modifies the RTC self‑test suite to detect whether a real‑time clock device is present; if not, the test is skipped instead of being marked as failed.
The commit removes a dead store assignment to the variable 'bytes' in the ALSA echoaudio MIDI driver, eliminating an unused write that caused a clang static analysis warning.

The commit modifies the kernel configuration so that the rcuscale tool can be built without automatically pulling in the RCU Tasks subsystem on CONFIG_PREEMPT_NONE kernels, reducing kernel size and tracing complexity.
The commit corrects a duplicated phrase "be split be split" to "be split" in the spte.h header of the KVM x86 MMU code, removing a typo/redundancy.

The commit reverts a previous change that populated the DCN315 clock table based on DCFCLK. The revert is needed because when DCFCLK states are disabled, the firmware (pmfw) leaves the table empty, causing a no‑read/underflow situation. Removing the DCFCLK‑based population restores correct behavior until a better solution is implemented.
Adds a compatible string entry for the Renesas RZ/V2L (R9A07G054) SoC in the Mali‑Bifrost GPU device‑tree bindings documentation, updating the dt‑bindings files.

The commit renames the struct pcie_port to dw_pcie_rp in the DesignWare PCIe core driver to align with the existing naming convention (dw_ prefix) used for other driver entities, improving code consistency and readability.
The commit updates the printk format string in amd-xgbe driver to use %x instead of %hx, matching the integer promotion rules and silencing a clang -Wformat warning.

The commit adjusts the MIPS jump_label code by casting the upper bound of the branch range to a signed long before performing the comparison. This ensures the range check uses signed arithmetic, preventing negative offsets from incorrectly satisfying the condition and triggering a warning.

The commit removes the HW_VAR_H2C_FW_P2P_PS_OFFLOAD case from the SetHwReg8188EU() function in the r8188eu driver, because that case only called another function. The change calls the function directly where needed and eliminates the redundant case, as part of cleaning up the unwanted HAL layer.

The commit adds the 'static' qualifier to symbols in arch/arm64/kernel/cpu_errata.c that are not meant to be globally visible, in order to silence sparse static analysis warnings about undeclared symbols.

The commit updates the device‑tree binding documentation for Qualcomm MSM display (MDSS) nodes. It removes an interrupt‑flags cell from the consumer side because the MDSS interrupt provider supplies only a single interrupt number, making the flags cell invalid.
The commit adds allocation of memory for array fields inside the struct ctrl_ref used by V4L2 controls, preparing the kernel to allow drivers to change the array size later.
The commit extends the Qualcomm socinfo driver by adding entries for additional SoCs (SM8350, SC8280XP, SA8540P, SM8450) and related PMICs, allowing the kernel to recognize these platforms.
The commit updates the rtl8xxxu Wi‑Fi driver to handle all chip version letters from A (0) to P (15) instead of treating versions beyond E as "unknown", expanding the recognized range of chip cuts.

The commit removes the .is_enabled field and its associated custom software enable count from the PRCMU clock driver in the ux500 platform, because the generic clock core already tracks enable counts. This simplifies the driver by deleting redundant state.

The commit reorganizes the use of CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL conditionals in the RISC-V memory initialization code. It groups related macros and moves the pt_ops macro next to its #ifdef, aiming to reduce scattered #ifdefs and improve code readability and maintainability.

The commit updates the device tree for the Qualcomm SM6350 (Lena) ARM64 platform, turning on the QUP and GPI DMA hardware blocks so that I2C devices that rely on those DMA engines can be added and function later.
The commit updates comment text in the rzg2l thermal driver: replaces an unclear label with a clearer one, corrects a spelling typo, and adjusts comment formatting to follow coding style guidelines.

The commit modifies the igc driver to detect failure when acquiring the hardware semaphore during release_swfw_sync, logs an error, and exits the loop to avoid an infinite loop that can happen on surprise removal of the hardware.
The commit updates the pcm3168a ASoC driver to replace old bus‑clock defines with the newer ASoC DAI format terminology, aligning the driver with current kernel coding conventions.
The commit deletes a redundant IS_SWAPFILE check in the NFS code because the macro is always defined, removing dead code and simplifying the implementation.

The commit adds kernel support for handling GTP echo requests. When a GTP_ECHO_REQ packet is received, the kernel now generates a GTP_ECHO_RSP reply containing the required GTPIE_RECOVERY information element and appropriate restart counter values for GTPv0 and GTPv1.

The commit replaces uses of cpumask_next_* with cpumask_first_* where the start index is -1 (i.e., start == 0), because cpumask_first() is a more efficient equivalent. It also introduces a missing helper cpumask_first_zero(). The change is purely internal, improving code efficiency and readability.

The commit removes the driver’s attempt to query TIM information via the "dtim_assoc" IOVAR, which is no longer supported by the firmware. This stops the driver from repeatedly issuing a failing call that produced noisy error messages after a Wi‑Fi roam.

The commit introduces a new board configuration (adl_rt5682) for Intel ASoC platforms that use the alc5682 headset codec without an external speaker amplifier, wiring the codec to SSP0 following the Intel Bluetooth offload design.

The commit adds a check in the io_uring networking path so that zerocopy sendmsg operations are rejected when the underlying socket does not support zerocopy, mirroring the behavior already applied to zerocopy send requests.
The commit modifies the documentation build preamble to check for the presence of xeCJK.sty before loading it, preventing LaTeX errors on systems where the texlive-xecjk package is missing.

The commit modifies the checkpatch script to stop flagging macros that contain a 'case' statement (e.g., #define C2S(x) case x: return #x) as errors about missing parentheses, because such macros are widely used in drivers.

The commit switches the simpledrm driver from the legacy simple‑KMS helper functions to the standard DRM atomic helper functions, removes empty CRTC enable/disable helpers, and forces a plane‑state check. No functional behavior changes are introduced.
The commit introduces a JPEG decoding worker interface in the MediaTek JPEG decoder driver, allowing three hardware decoder units on the MT8195 SoC to operate in parallel.

The commit removes unnecessary VPRINTK debug logging calls and the ata_scsi_dump_cdb() helper from libata because the same information is already provided by existing tracepoints, cleaning up dead code.

Adds logic to the AMD DRM display driver to calculate extra vblank time when entering FREESYNC_STATE_VIDEO and, if the gained time exceeds 5 ms, enables entry into zstate, updating relevant registers and restoring defaults on exit.

The commit corrects the prototype of the hqd_destroy() callback in the AMDGPU KFD driver. The third argument, reset_type, was declared as enum kfd_preempt_type but the actual implementations use uint32_t, causing a Control Flow Integrity (CFI) type mismatch when compiled with CONFIG_CFI_CLANG. The change updates the function pointer type to uint32_t, eliminating the CFI violation on affected hardware.

The commit updates the device-tree binding documentation for STM32 pinctrl. It changes the property name from the incorrect 'reset' to the correct 'resets' and adds 'additionalProperties: false' to the schema, fixing the binding definition.
The commit modifies the ath9k driver to suppress a false array‑bounds warning emitted by GCC 12, likely by adjusting warning flags or adding a pragma, without changing driver functionality.
The commit updates the sun8i-ss crypto driver to replace calls to pm_runtime_get_sync() with pm_runtime_resume_and_get(), which better matches the intended usage and simplifies the driver code.

The commit reorganizes io_uring code by moving the fixed file installation and removal helpers into the filetable subsystem and changes their signatures to accept a context (ctx) instead of an io_kiocb/request, reflecting a cleaner architectural placement.

The commit updates the device‑tree binding documentation for the NXP i.MX8MQ VPU, separating the previously combined G1 and G2 decoder blocks into distinct nodes because they are independent and controlled by the vpu-blk-ctrl power‑domain driver.

The commit removes the IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON macro from the iwlwifi driver configuration because it is only used once inside the IWL_DEVICE_BZ macro, and merges its contents directly into that macro, simplifying the code.
